2606.27793.title: Position Bias Correction is Insufficient for One-Pass Attention Sorting
2606.27793.paper_summary: Long-context language models suffer from position bias, where information in middle positions is underutilized. Attention Sorting addresses this by iteratively reordering documents based on attention patterns, but its multiple sort-and-generate cycles increase deployment cost. We hypothesize that position bias is the primary bottleneck and propose Debiased One-Pass Attention Sorting, which estimates a per-prompt position-bias curve from the low-attention majority of documents and uses it to correct raw attention scores (via subtraction or division) to enable single-pass sorting. Our experiments on two models refute this hypothesis in the tested setting: on LLaMA-2-7B-32K-Instruct, debiasing produces identical results to uncalibrated single-pass sorting (94.83\% containment accuracy), while on YaRN-Llama-2-7b-64k, debiasing improves accuracy by 8.67 percentage points but remains 14.84pp behind iterative sorting, closing only 37\% of the gap. These results suggest that position-bias correction is insufficient to match iterative sorting, and that repeated reordering provides additional benefits beyond bias correction.
2606.27793.paper_summary_zh: 長文語言模型受到位置偏見的影響，中間位置的信息未被充分利用。注意力排序通過根據注意力模式反覆重新排序文檔來解決這個問題，但其多次排序和生成循環增加了部署成本。我們假設位置偏見是主要瓶頸，並提出去偏見的一次性注意力排序，該方法從低注意力的文檔中估計每個提示的位置偏見曲線，並利用它來修正原始注意力分數（通過減法或除法）以實現單次排序。我們在兩個模型上的實驗駁斥了這一假設：在LLaMA-2-7B-32K-Instruct上，去偏見產生的結果與未校準的單次排序相同（94.83\%的包含準確率），而在YaRN-Llama-2-7b-64k上，去偏見提高了8.67個百分點的準確率，但仍然比迭代排序落後14.84個百分點，只縮小了37\%的差距。這些結果表明，位置偏見的修正不足以與迭代排序相匹配，並且重複的重新排序提供了超出偏見修正的額外好處。
2606.27793.author: Qiong Tang et.al.
2606.27793.authors: Qiong Tang, Xiangkun Hu, Xiangyang Liu, Yiran Chen, Yunfan Shao
